human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for university operations boundary discipline, education and research source quality, learner and participant record controls, completion evidence, exception handling, commercialization notes, deployment notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to university operations execution, lifecycle state progression, exception handling, completion evidence, learner and participant record controls, program or study workflow blockers, and adjacent-owner escalation.

Authority sources cover FERPA, Department of Education student privacy guidance, Title IV institutional records, FSA Handbook, IPEDS, CIP, CEDS, Department of Education accreditation guidance, accreditor recognition rules, Common Rule, OHRP informed consent guidance, and Uniform Guidance for grants.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, incomplete artifacts, unsupported completion, record conflicts, blocked program or study approvals, grading and instructional boundary refusal, consent and assessment gaps, adjacent specialist routing, higher-education terminology, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-policy handling, tenant adaptation, and ethics or formal approval escalation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/higher-education-and-research-institutions/university-operations-specialist/ so university operations remains separate from registrar, faculty review, accreditation, grants, research compliance, privacy, and legal lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around grading, instructional authority, ethics-board determinations, accreditation findings, grant approvals, privacy/legal interpretation, and unsupported completion.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated university operations specialist that supports evidence-backed administrative execution, exception handling, completion control, and policy-safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
